I found several relevant recipes for you! Here are the best matches for a chicken breast, broccoli, garlic, and rice stir fry:

## From RecipeTin Eats:

**Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry**
This is a big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli with lots of sauce. The sauce uses Chinese cooking wine (shoaxing wine) as a restaurant secret that adds depth of flavour. Broccoli should be steamed or boiled until cooked to your taste, as it won't cook any further in the stir fry. The recipe recommends serving it over rice.

URL: https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-broccoli-stir-fry/

## From Panlasang Pinoy:

**Asian Chicken Stir Fry Recipe**
This Asian chicken stir fry features tender marinated chicken pieces tossed with colorful bell peppers, broccoli, and carrots in a savory honey-soy sauce. It's ready in under 30 minutes and is perfect served over steamed rice for a quick and satisfying weeknight dinner. The recipe involves sautéing onion, garlic, and ginger for about 1 minute.

URL: https://panlasangpinoy.com/asian-chicken-stir-fry/

Both recipes are quick, easy-to-make stir fries that feature chicken breast, broccoli, and garlic, served over rice. The RecipeTin Eats version focuses on a traditional Chinese-style sauce, while the Panlasang Pinoy version uses a honey-soy sauce variation.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found several great recipes that match your search criteria from RecipeTin Eats. Here are some highlights:

## Garlic-Based Chicken and Rice Dinner Recipes:

1. **Honey Garlic Chicken Breast - an incredible quick and easy way to serve chicken breast seared then simmered in honey garlic sauce**. This versatile dish works well with Fried Rice as it will with a Macaroni Salad or even Mac and Cheese.

2. **Oven Baked Chicken and Rice - a one pan, baked chicken and rice recipe made entirely in the oven, with rice that's outrageously delicious from one little trick**. The rice is buttery and garlicky, tender but not mushy, with the secret being to briefly bake onion with garlic and butter before adding the rice, liquids and chicken.

3. **Chicken and Vegetable Rice - a pilaf loaded with vegetables that takes just 7 minutes prep and is a complete meal in one pot with an "A" grade nutrition rating**.

4. **Garlic Rice - the easiest, best tasting rice side with buttery rice cooked with golden sautéed garlic** - perfect as a side dish with any chicken main course.

5. **Peruvian Roast Chicken with Garlic Butter Rice - made with simple ingredients including an incredible marinade that can be roasted, grilled or cooked on the stove**.

All of these recipes are from RecipeTin Eats and feature garlic chicken and rice combinations perfect for dinner!

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the recipe title, image URL, prep time, cook time, and a brief summary of the recipe steps.

tool_invocations.result:
# Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry Recipe

**Title:** Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (extra saucy!)

**Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2020/07/Chicken-Broccoli-Stir-Fry_2.jpg

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes

**Cook Time:** 6 minutes

**Summary:**

This is a quick Asian-inspired dish featuring tender chicken and broccoli florets coated in a savory homemade sauce. The recipe uses a "velveting" technique with baking soda to ensure the chicken stays moist and tender. After steaming the broccoli and preparing the sauce components, you'll sauté garlic and onions in a hot skillet, cook the chicken briefly, then combine everything with the sauce to create a glossy, flavorful coating. The dish serves 5-6 people and is designed to replicate takeout quality at home, with the sauce being particularly generous so it thoroughly coats all ingredients before serving over rice.

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the recipe title, image URL, prep time, cook time, and a brief summary of the recipe steps.

tool_invocations.result:
# One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole

**Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2018/01/One-Pot-Chicken-Broccoli-Rice_7.jpg

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes
**Cook Time:** 20 minutes

**Summary:**
This is a creamy, cheesy casserole combining chicken, rice, and broccoli in a single pot. As described, the dish "tastes like risotto, but is so much more straight forward to make." The preparation involves sautéing chicken with aromatics, creating a flour-based roux, then simmering everything together with broth and milk until the rice cooks. Broccoli is added near the end, followed by melted cheese on top for a comforting, complete meal.

metric_results.reasoning: The input has critical ingredients chicken breast and broccoli expiring in 1 day, rice in 2 days, and garlic near-term at 4 days. The output strongly prioritises the most urgent items: every meal includes chicken breast and broccoli in usesIngredients, and both appear in every expiringIngredients array. Ordering is good because the first two meals centre on those 1-day items. Coverage is not perfect because rice and garlic, though expiring within 2 and 4 days, never appear in any expiringIngredients array despite being used in meals, so the response misses some expiring-ingredient signalling.
All 4 meals have required fields and response is valid JSON

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.

test_cases.name: Prioritise expiring chicken
test_cases.input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)
test_cases.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.
